text:
Add implementation plans to Phase 6 (Backup/Rotate) and Phase 7 (Polish) tasks. Run these commands:

bd update SM-1i3.3 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (get.go — add --clip flag)
- Use backend.NewClipboardBackend().Copy(value)
- Print "Copied to clipboard" to stderr only
- Never print secret value to stdout when --clip used
- Re-auth if session expired
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.2 (get command), SM-coy.1 or SM-ckp (clipboard backend)
- Acceptance: --clip copies to clipboard, stderr message, no stdout output" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.2 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (edit.go)
- Temp file: ioutil.TempFile(os.Getenv("HOME")+"/.envmoat", "edit-"), mode 0600
- Write current value to temp file
- exec.Command(os.Getenv("EDITOR") "vim"), temp file)
- On exit code 0: read new value, update bundle, atomic write
- On non-zero: no changes, no error
- Cleanup: defer os.Remove(tempPath)
- Re-auth if session expired
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.2 (set/get), SM-gtv.7 (encryption)
- Acceptance: edit opens editor, saves on exit, temp file cleaned up, no changes on cancel" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.1 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (backup.go, restore.go)
- backup --to-file: decrypt all bundles with current LUK, write to single file
- Format: [4B magic "EMBT"][4B version][varint bundle count][for each: varint name len, name, varint data len, encrypted data]
- Re-encrypt each bundle with a backup-key derived from LUK via HKDF
- restore --from-file: parse format, decrypt with current LUK, merge into existing bundles
- Conflict prompt: skip/overwrite/cancel per key
- 'export' alias for backup, 'import' alias for restore
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.7 (encryption), SM-ckp or SM-coy (auth)
- Acceptance: backup creates valid file, restore merges correctly, conflict prompts work" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.4 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.4 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (rotate.go)
- Phase 1: decrypt all bundles with current LUK -> temp directory
- Phase 2: prompt for new password (confirm match), derive new LUK, re-encrypt all bundles
- Atomic: rename temp bundles to final, keep .bak of old bundles
- Rollback: on any failure, restore .bak files
- Progress: "Rotating bundle 1/3..."
- Keychain: delete old items (SecItemDelete), create new with new password
- Message: "Rotated N bundles. Run any command to authenticate."
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.7 (encryption), SM-ckp or SM-coy (keychain)
- Acceptance: all bundles re-encrypted, old keychain items deleted, .bak rollback on failure" 2>&1

bd update SM-rgt.3 --description "$(bd show SM-rgt.3 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Create asdf-envmoat plugin repo (separate or embedded)
- Files: bin/install, bin/list-all-versions, bin/latest-version
- install: download GitHub release binary for $(uname -s)/$(uname -m)
- Fallback: go build from source
- Version resolution: git tags via gh api or git ls-remote
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.6 (scaffolding complete)
- Acceptance: asdf install envmoat latest works, binary runs" 2>&1

bd update SM-rgt.4 --description "$(bd show SM-rgt.4 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Create mise plugin specification
- Files: mise.toml or .mise.toml in plugin repo
- Binary download: GitHub releases with OS/arch detection
- Build-from-source fallback
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.6 (scaffolding complete)
- Acceptance: mise install envmoat works, binary runs" 2>&1

bd update SM-rgt.2 --description "$(bd show SM-rgt.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (completions.go)
- cobra: rootCmd.GenZshCompletion(os.Stdout), rootCmd.GenBashCompletion(os.Stdout)
- Command: envmoat completions <zsh|bash>
- Dynamic completions: profile names (from index.json), key names (from current bundle)
- Install hints: print path for zsh (~/.zfunc/_envmoat) and bash (~/.bash_completion)
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.6 (scaffolding)
- Acceptance: zsh completion script valid, bash completion script valid, dynamic completions work" 2>&1

bd update SM-rgt.5 --description "$(bd show SM-rgt.5 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- CI: GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/release.yml
- Steps: go build -> codesign sign -> xcrun notarytool submit -> xcrun stapler staple
- codesign: codesign --sign "Developer ID Application: ..." --entitlements entitlements.plist --options runtime envmoat
- notarization: xcrun notarytool submit envmoat.zip --keychain-profile "NotaryDistribution"
- Entitlements: none needed (no TCC access)
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.6 (scaffolding)
- Acceptance: signed binary passes Gatekeeper, notarized, staple attached" 2>&1

bd update SM-rgt.1 --description "$(bd show SM-rgt.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- CI: .github/workflows/ci.yml
- go mod verify in CI
- govulncheck ./... (golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck)
- Reproducible builds: GOFLAGS=-buildvcs=false, compare checksums
- Release: SHA256SUMS file, gpg signature
- Pin GitHub Actions versions to full commit SHAs
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.6 (scaffolding)
- Acceptance: CI passes go mod verify, govulncheck clean, reproducible build checksums match" 2>&1

echo "Done updating Phase 6 and 7 tasks."

---
Update progress at: /Users/cameronlockhart/Development/secrets-manager/.pi-subagents/artifacts/progress/222a0ee2/progress.md

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: reviewed
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Implement the requested change without widening scope
- criterion-2: Return evidence sufficient for an independent acceptance review

Required evidence: changed-files, tests-added, commands-run, validation-output, residual-risks, no-staged-files

Review gate: required by reviewer.

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: reviewed
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

Criteria:
- criterion-1: Implement the requested change without widening scope
- criterion-2: Return evidence sufficient for an independent acceptance review

Required evidence: changed-files, tests-added, commands-run, validation-output, residual-risks, no-staged-files

Review gate: required by reviewer.

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:
Use empty arrays when no items apply; array fields contain strings unless object entries are shown.
```acceptance-report
{
  "criteriaSatisfied": [
    {
      "id": "criterion-1",
      "status": "satisfied",
      "evidence": "specific proof"
    }
  ],
  "changedFiles": [
    "src/file.ts"
  ],
  "testsAddedOrUpdated": [
    "test/file.test.ts"
  ],
  "commandsRun": [
    {
      "command": "command",
      "result": "passed",
      "summary": "short result"
    }
  ],
  "validationOutput": [
    "validation output or concise summary"
  ],
  "residualRisks": [
    "none"
  ],
  "noStagedFiles": true,
  "diffSummary": "short description of the diff",
  "reviewFindings": [
    "blocker: file.ts:12 - issue found, or no blockers"
  ],
  "manualNotes": "anything else the parent should know"
}
```

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (get.go — add --clip flag)
- Use backend.NewClipboardBackend().Copy(value)
- Print "Copied to clipboard" to stderr only
- Never print secret value to stdout when --clip used
- Re-auth if session expired
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.2 (get command), SM-coy.1 or SM-ckp (clipboard backend)
- Acceptance: --clip copies to clipboard, stderr message, no stdout output" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.2 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.2 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (edit.go)
- Temp file: ioutil.TempFile(os.Getenv("HOME")+"/.envmoat", "edit-"), mode 0600
- Write current value to temp file
- exec.Command(os.Getenv("EDITOR") "vim"), temp file)
- On exit code 0: read new value, update bundle, atomic write
- On non-zero: no changes, no error
- Cleanup: defer os.Remove(tempPath)
- Re-auth if session expired
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.2 (set/get), SM-gtv.7 (encryption)
- Acceptance: edit opens editor, saves on exit, temp file cleaned up, no changes on cancel" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.1 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.1 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (backup.go, restore.go)
- backup --to-file: decrypt all bundles with current LUK, write to single file
- Format: [4B magic "EMBT"][4B version][varint bundle count][for each: varint name len, name, varint data len, encrypted data]
- Re-encrypt each bundle with a backup-key derived from LUK via HKDF
- restore --from-file: parse format, decrypt with current LUK, merge into existing bundles
- Conflict prompt: skip/overwrite/cancel per key
- 'export' alias for backup, 'import' alias for restore
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.7 (encryption), SM-ckp or SM-coy (auth)
- Acceptance: backup creates valid file, restore merges correctly, conflict prompts work" 2>&1

bd update SM-1i3.4 --description "$(bd show SM-1i3.4 --json | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)[0]['description'])")

## Implementation Plan
- Package: cmd/ (rotate.go)
- Phase 1: decrypt all bundles with current LUK -> temp directory
- Phase 2: prompt for new password (confirm match), derive new LUK, re-encrypt all bundles
- Atomic: rename temp bundles to final, keep .bak of old bundles
- Rollback: on any failure, restore .bak files
- Progress: "Rotating bundle 1/3..."
- Keychain: delete old items (SecItemDelete), create new with new password
- Message: "Rotated N bundles. Run any command to authenticate."
- Dependencies: SM-gtv.7 (encryption), SM-ckp or SM-coy (keychain)
- Acceptance: all bundles re-encrypted, old keychain items deleted, .bak rollback on failure" 2>&1

All three Phase 1 infra tasks now have detailed implementation plans appended via `bd update`. Plans include package structure, files, dependencies, and acceptance criteria aligned with PLAN.md encryption model (scrypt N=262144, HKDF-SHA256, AES-256-GCM, file format [0x01][12B nonce][ciphertext][32B auth tag]).

## Phase 1 CLI Implementation Plans Added

- **SM-gtv.5** (setup + init): Implementation plan appended covering cmd/ package structure, password setup via golang.org/x/term, salt generation, shell hook install (zsh chpwd + bash PROMPT_COMMAND), --reset flag, init marker/bundle creation, auto-bundle naming, .gitignore append
- **SM-gtv.2** (set + get + list): Implementation plan appended covering set/get/list commands, interactive prompts, --stdin/--file flags, key validation regex, 1MB size warning, context resolution via resolver
- **SM-gtv.3** (load + remove + deinit + verify): Implementation plan appended covering shell-safe output (single-quote escaping), bundle hash line, remove/deinit with confirmation, verify orphan detection, -y/--yes flag

All three `bd update` commands succeeded. PLAN.md lines 100-200 reviewed for shell hook details (zsh chpwd hook, bash PROMPT_COMMAND, shell-safe output format with single-quote escaping, bundle hash change detection).
